findings.evidence: Mathlib.RingTheory.Bialgebra.TensorProduct.lean:68-72 defines `Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map` under `[Bialgebra S C] [Bialgebra R D]`, while Mathlib.RingTheory.Coalgebra.TensorProduct.lean:69-70 proves `TensorProduct.counit_tmul` under `CoalgebraStruct`; Hopf only adds antipode axioms in Mathlib.RingTheory.HopfAlgebra.Basic.lean:65-75.
findings.fix: Split the file into weaker sections, using `[Bialgebra k A] [Bialgebra k B] [Bialgebra k C]` for `baseChangeMap`/`id`/`comp`/`tmul`, and weaken the counit lemma to the coalgebra or at most bialgebra hypotheses needed.
findings.issue: The shared variable block requires `[HopfAlgebra k A] [HopfAlgebra k B] [HopfAlgebra k C]`, but `baseChangeMap` and its functoriality lemmas only use bialgebra structure, and `baseChange_counit_tmul` only uses the tensor-product counit formula.

findings.evidence: Line 60 states `baseChangeMap f (x ⊗ₜ a) = x ⊗ₜ f a`; line 95 restates only the `x = 1` case.
findings.fix: Remove `baseChangeMap_one_tmul`; users can get the same result from `simp` using `baseChangeMap_tmul` with `x = 1`.
findings.issue: `baseChangeMap_one_tmul` is a special case of the immediately preceding general `[simp]` lemma `baseChangeMap_tmul`, so it duplicates API at a less general level.

summary: The new API is mostly at the intended base-change level, but it imposes Hopf hypotheses on declarations whose natural Mathlib level is bialgebra or coalgebra. It also exports a redundant special-case map lemma already covered by the general tensor lemma.
